name: Corrigo Facilities Management API
what_it_does: Corrigo facilities management and work order REST API for corporate real estate, facilities managers, and retail chains to manage work orders, service vendor dispatch, and facility maintenance across multi-site portfolios. Enables AI agents to manage work order creation and routing for facilities service request automation, handle service vendor assignment and dispatch for facilities contractor management automation, access equipment and asset tracking for facilities asset management automation, retrieve service level agreement monitoring and exception alerting for FM performance automation, manage preventive maintenance scheduling for building systems management automation, handle invoice processing and cost tracking for facilities spend management automation, access multi-location facility portfolio management for corporate real estate operations automation, retrieve reporting and analytics for portfolio facilities performance automation, manage tenant request intake for commercial real estate tenant services automation, and integrate Corrigo with JLL Tango (space management), ERP, and building management systems for end-to-end corporate facilities management.
use_cases: Dispatching facility work orders from corporate FM automation agents
Managing service vendor performance from facilities analytics agents
Processing maintenance invoices from FM spend management agents
Tracking SLA compliance from facilities performance agents
not_for: Manufacturing plant maintenance (use Fiix or IBM Maximo)
Residential property management (use Buildium or AppFolio)
Space utilization and workplace management (use Condeco or Tango)
best_when: A corporate real estate team, retail FM operator, or facilities management company wanting AI agents to automate work order routing, service vendor dispatch, SLA monitoring, and facilities spend management within Corrigo's multi-site FM platform.
avoid_when: VENDOR CONTRACTOR LICENSE VERIFICATION FOR REGULATED TRADES: Automated service vendor dispatch via Corrigo for regulated facility trades (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, fire safety) must verify contractor license and insurance currency before dispatch; automated dispatch of unlicensed contractor creates client liability and regulatory violation. SLA BREACH PENALTY AUTOMATED DEDUCTION: Automated SLA performance monitoring via Corrigo that triggers penalty deductions for vendor SLA breach must comply with facility services contract terms; automated deduction without vendor notification and cure period creates contract dispute risk. TENANT COMMUNICATION FOR COMMERCIAL FACILITIES: Automated tenant service request routing via Corrigo must comply with commercial lease communication requirements; automated work order notification that discloses building system condition information beyond tenant's space may violate landlord confidentiality obligations.

auth.notes: Corrigo uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Dallas, Texas HQ. Founded 2001. Acquired by JLL (Jones Lang LaSalle, NYSE: JLL) in 2015. JLL's proprietary FM technology platform. Work order management, vendor dispatch, preventive maintenance, and FM analytics. Used by retail chains, corporate real estate, and healthcare facility management. Integrates with JLL Tango (space management) and Oracle ERP. Competes with ServiceChannel, Archibus, and Planon for multi-site facilities management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise subscription for corporate FM programs
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription based on location count and user seats; API access included in enterprise engagement; pricing negotiated as part of JLL facilities management engagement
pricing.notes: Dallas TX. JLL (NYSE: JLL) subsidiary. Annual subscription. Location-count-based pricing. Often bundled with JLL FM services. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; work order and vendor queries throttled; webhooks for work order and SLA events

known_agent_gotchas: JLL TANGO INTEGRATION DEPENDENCY FOR SPACE CONTEXT: Corrigo work orders may require space and location context from JLL Tango (space management); automated work order creation for clients using Tango must include Tango location ID; automated work order without Tango-linked location creates work order with unresolvable location reference for facilities with Tango as location master

VENDOR PREFERRED PROVIDER LIST ENFORCEMENT: Automated service vendor dispatch via Corrigo must query preferred provider list (PPL) before assignment; automated dispatch to non-PPL vendor without approved exception creates procurement policy violation for corporate clients with mandatory PPL requirements; implement PPL check in automated dispatch logic

SLA CLOCK START VS ACKNOWLEDGMENT: Corrigo SLA clock behavior varies by work order type — some SLAs start at work order creation, others at vendor acknowledgment; automated SLA performance calculation must use correct SLA clock start per work order type; automated SLA reporting with incorrect clock start creates vendor performance distortion

INVOICE APPROVAL WORKFLOW THRESHOLD: Automated invoice processing via Corrigo must apply authorization threshold for approval routing; automated invoice approval without spend limit validation creates unauthorized payments above delegated authority; configure automated invoice approval with purchase order match and spend authority check

JLL ACQUISITION PLATFORM LOCK-IN: Corrigo is exclusively a JLL product; non-JLL FM clients using Corrigo API build dependency on JLL technology ecosystem; evaluate vendor lock-in risk before building deep automated integration with Corrigo for facilities not managed by JLL

MULTI-SITE LOCATION HIERARCHY FOR CORPORATE PORTFOLIOS: Corrigo multi-site location hierarchy must be correctly mapped before automated work order routing; automated work order creation without correct location hierarchy mapping routes work orders to wrong regional vendor pool; validate location hierarchy configuration for each new facility before automated work order deployment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Facilities management. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Work order data, vendor information, and facility operations records.

security_score_components.security_notes: FM platform. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Work order, vendor, and facility operations data.
